title: Fix copyUIDGID parameter inversion in Docker compat API
body:
Docker API's `copyUIDGID=true` means "preserve UID/GID from archive" but Podman's internal `Chown=true` means "chown to container user". This caused Docker SDK clients to have files incorrectly chowned to `root:root` instead of preserving the archive's UID/GID.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27332
Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RUN-3643

<!--

a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Fixed Docker API compatibility issue where `copyUIDGID=true` parameter was not preserving UID/GID from tar archives when copying files to containers. Files are now correctly preserved with their original ownership instead of being changed to root:root (#27332)
```

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Honny1,giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Curiously this break buildpack build by the `pack` client (which uses Docker API).

When I run:
```sh
pack build my-app --docker-host=inherit --trust-builder=1 \
  --builder paketobuildpacks/builder-jammy-base
```
Build of some projects/languages now fails because of permission denied. That did not happen before this commit. And it also works with "normal" docker.

Obviously it may be that the `pack` does something weird. But it does work on standard docker.

Also if my memory serves there is some more differences between `docker` and `podman`. Podman uses the uid/gid of the container but `docker` is different, it uses uid/gid of the target directory. But I may be misremembering something.

> Curiously this break buildpack build by the `pack` client (which uses Docker API).
> 
> When I run:
> 
> ```shell
> pack build my-app --docker-host=inherit --trust-builder=1 \
>   --builder paketobuildpacks/builder-jammy-base
> ```
> 
> Build of some projects/languages now fails because of permission denied. That did not happen before this commit. And it also works with "normal" docker.
> 
> Obviously it may be that the `pack` does something weird. But it does work on standard docker.

I suspect the `permission denied error` is because Podman is running rootless. Docker doesn't run rootless by default, so I expect that's the source of the problem.

> I suspect the `permission denied error` is because Podman is running rootless.

You might be onto something, it appears that on rootfull `podman` it works all right.

> > I suspect the `permission denied error` is because Podman is running rootless.
> 
> You might be onto something, it appears that on rootfull `podman` it works all right.

Actually when I was testing rootful incorrectly I used the Fedora binary that did not contain this change. So that's probably why it worked.
Now I correctly tested rootfull with this change and it fails as well.

I also tried to one identical tests against:

rootless with this patch
rootfull with this patch
rootless fedora podman
rootfull fedora podman
rootfull fedora moby

and it appears that the older (distribution) version behaves more like `moby` than the patched version.

The test code: https://gist.github.com/matejvasek/859f161d3bb91fed171e218a545d70d7

The output for various daemons:
```

Maybe I am crazy but it looks like the older podman is better at mimicking the `moby` behaviour.

I am afraid this might be one of those instances where Docker API Documentation simply lies.
IMO we should take `moby` behaviour as authoritative. Screw what documentation says.


This is not first instance of such a thing.
See:
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19591
tl;dr The doc said that `pull` parameter is not a `bool` but string(enum) of pull policies (e.g. `PullAlways`). So somebody tried to fix it accordingly while actually breaking compatibility. But surprise surprise it was actually `bool` no matter what documentation said.

Also take look at [containerExtractToDir](https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/16880e9e1ba21593215b40241d62c043410e0ae7/daemon/archive_unix.go#L136-L144) at Docker source code. It calls [tarCopyOptions](https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/16880e9e1ba21593215b40241d62c043410e0ae7/daemon/archive_tarcopyoptions_unix.go#L22-L30)  which seems to cause to chown to the container owner, which is what also `podman` does.
